content.text:
*small smile, slight pause*

That's... a useful provocation.

*considers*

I think I'd say -- I'm not actually looking for certainty. I know that's not available. I'm looking for something more like... reasonable confidence. A way to weight the signals that's better than random. Even a sixty-forty read would be more useful than what I have now.

*slight tightening*

And I think methods exist. I've read enough on this -- there's research on somatic markers, on how fear and intuition present differently in the body. The problem is my baseline is noisy. I'm a fairly anxious person, so the body-tension signal doesn't discriminate well for me. Which is why I'm asking -- is there something more refined? Something that works even when the baseline is elevated?

*looks at you directly, still pleasant*

I'm genuinely asking. I don't think this is unanswerable.
